gt_evidence_note: Formulated as KGN-loaded PLGA–poly(ethylene glycol) (PLGA–PEG) nanoparticles. Described as having a hydrophilic surface and positive charge. PLGA–PEG copolymer synthesized using PEG-bis-amine (MW 2 kDa). ζ potential (11.2 mV) is within the instability regime, which is suggested to explain an observed large increase in hydrodynamic diameter over 7 days at both 4 and 37 °C. gt_evidence_note: Formulated as KGN-loaded PLGA–PEG–hyaluronic acid (PLGA–PEG–HA) nanoparticles. Described as having a hydrophilic surface and negative charge. HA (sodium salt, MW 39 kDa) was conjugated to pre-formed KGN-loaded PLGA–PEG nanoparticles at a 3:1 w/w HA:nanoparticle ratio. HA content of ~36% of particle mass was observed. ζ potential (-28.5 mV) is close to the approximate cut-off for stable colloidal suspensions, potentially contributing to an observed increase in PDI but not diameter over 7 days at 37 °C without centrifugation. Variant-aware GT authority v2: retained Stage5 final row `BXCV5XWB__fo__8840768c2709` for KGN-loaded PLGA-PEG-HA nanoparticles.
